deff_comparison.note: AR(1) ρ=0.2583 → Deff_AR(m̄)=1.6584 (asymptote 1.6966); the MEASURED ACF decays slower than ρ^k (ρ(2)=0.168 ≫ ρ(1)²=0.067), so the empirical-ACF Deff 2.7743 > AR(1) 1.6584. Both ≪ the flat-exchangeable 4.4106. AR(1) is the optimistic lower bound; the measured ACF is the data-grounded realistic Deff; flat 4.41 is the conservative ceiling.

savings_invariance.note: N_fss/E[N] = 75.12× is Deff-invariant: both the fixed-N reference and the SPRT E[N] scale by the same cluster Deff. The AR correction tightens the ABSOLUTE realism band (flat → AR / measured-ACF) but leaves the 75× collapse untouched.

handoff_lines.fern_185: fern #185: the AR(1)-corrected liveprobe certification cost is E[N]_nogo=672 (AR(1) optimistic lower bound) / 1,125 (measured-ACF, data-grounded), vs #205's conservative flat-Deff 1,788 and the IID floor 405; realized (0.05,0.95) unchanged. Carry the tightened expected-N band [582 tight … 1,788 flat-loose] as the measurement-cost row. The 75× collapse vs #197's fixed-N is Deff-INVARIANT (fixed-N and SPRT E[N] scale by the SAME cluster Deff), so the decaying-ACF correction leaves the 75× headline intact while SHARPENING the absolute realism band below the conservative flat 4.41; it never reverses the NO-GO-is-cheap conclusion. flat ×4.41 is the loose end.

operating_characteristic_invariance.note: the AR correction rescales the per-trial INFORMATION (variance), not the per-trial DRIFT or the Wald boundaries A,B — so the OC and realized (α,power) are unchanged; only the ASN E[N] is multiplied by Deff.
operating_characteristic_invariance.regime_caveat: E[N]·Deff is asymptotically exact in the DRIFT-dominated regime and one-sided conservative (overestimates) in the variance-dominated regime. The NO-GO truth (λ_eq=0.540) sits 20× the 0.022 margin below the indifference midpoint μ_mid=0.989 (P(decide GO)=5.36e-53) → strongly drift-dominated, so the TEST metric expected_n_nogo_ar is on solid ground. The near-bar (β=1) and worst-case (indifference) truths are variance-dominated, so their AR-corrected E[N] are CONSERVATIVE upper bounds — the realism tightening there is, if anything, understated.

realism_band.note: ρ(1)∈[0.1863,0.3317] → Deff_AR∈[1.4349,1.9322] → E[N]_nogo∈[582,783]. The loose AR end 1.932 and the measured-ACF Deff 2.774 both sit below the flat-exchangeable 4.411 → #205's flat ×4.41 (E[N]_nogo 1788) is the CONSERVATIVE (loose) end.
realism_band.rho_ci_method: inherits #190 prompt-level cluster-bootstrap ICC CI [0.1043,0.1857], scaled to lag-1 ACF (ρ∝corr-strength)

synthesis.verdict: AR-CORRECTED. #205 priced the liveprobe realism band with a CONSERVATIVE flat ×Deff=4.41 (exchangeable ICC=0.1446). Folding #190's DECAYING serial ACF (ρ(1)=0.2583) into the partial-sum variance gives Deff_AR(m̄)=1.658 (asymptote 1.697), a 2.66× tightening — E[N]_nogo drops from the flat 1,788 to 672. HONEST CAVEAT: the MEASURED ACF decays slower than pure AR(1), so the data-grounded empirical-ACF Deff 2.774 (E[N]_nogo 1,125, 1.59× tighter) is the realistic value; AR(1) is the optimistic lower bound. The ρ-CI band is [582,783]; even the loose end stays below the flat-Deff 1,788, so flat ×4.41 is the CONSERVATIVE (loose) end (flat_441_is_conservative=True). The decaying-ACF correction only SHARPENS #205's 75× collapse; realized (α,power)=(0.05,0.95) and the bar 0.9780 are untouched. NOT a launch.
